entries.d: very bad weather with strong wind and rain
entries.s: The trees fell down and windows broke during the big _____ last night with strong winds.

entries.d: a machine you wear on your back to fly
entries.s: The comic book hero put on his rocket-powered _____ on his back and flew up into the sky.

entries.d: a flying machine with blades on top
entries.s: The _____ with spinning blades on top landed on the flat roof of the hospital.

entries.d: people who help others at sea
entries.s: The _____ in their fast boat saved the swimmers from the rough sea water near the beach.

entries.d: a group that puts out fires
entries.s: Someone called the _____ on 999 when the old house was burning with orange flames.

entries.d: a car that takes sick people to hospital
entries.s: The white _____ with flashing blue lights came quickly to take the hurt boy with a broken leg to hospital.

entries.d: people who stop crime and keep us safe
entries.s: My mum called the _____ on her phone when she saw the masked robber with a knife at the shop.

entries.d: a team that saves people on mountains
entries.s: The brave _____ team with ropes and ice picks found the lost hikers in the deep snow on the mountain.

entries.d: to call out loudly because you need help
entries.s: She was lost in the dark forest and scared, so she started _____ as loud as she could with her voice.

entries.d: to have good things happen to you
entries.s: We found the very last two concert tickets at the desk. We were _____!

entries.d: to damage something so it is in pieces
entries.s: Be careful walking on the slippery ice or you might fall and _____ your arm in two pieces.

entries.d: a place like Austria, England, or France
entries.s: Italy is a large _____ in the south of Europe, with its capital Rome.

entries.d: something bad that is against the law
entries.s: Stealing money from a shop is a serious _____ and the police will arrest you.

entries.d: hot, bright flames that burn things
entries.s: We sat around the warm _____ with orange flames to stay warm on the cold night.

entries.d: something bad that happens by surprise
entries.s: There was a serious car _____ on the main road this morning. Two cars crashed into each other.

entries.d: to not be safe
entries.s: The small kitten stuck on the high roof was _____. It might fall down and hurt itself.

entries.d: to go up a mountain by flying
entries.s: The superhero man with the jetpack can _____ in just a few seconds without climbing.
mc: to slowly walk up the mountain

entries.d: to send a wireless voice message over the air
entries.s: The helicopter pilot had to _____ the rescue team on the ground for help with the emergency.

entries.d: a group of people who save others
entries.s: The _____ with first aid kits and ropes arrived in a helicopter to help us in the emergency.

entries.d: a hard, heavy piece of stone
entries.s: She sat on a big grey _____ — a large heavy stone — next to the flowing river.

entries.d: to fall because the ground is smooth or wet
entries.s: Be very careful! The wet kitchen floor is slippery and you could _____ and fall over.

entries.d: covered in water, not dry
entries.s: My socks and shoes are soaking _____ because I walked through the deep rain puddles outside.

entries.d: to come to a place
entries.s: What time does the train _____ at the busy station? I need to meet my grandma.

entries.d: to be away from danger
entries.s: The children are all inside the warm house now, away from the storm. They are _____.

entries.d: to see pictures in your head when you sleep
entries.s: I like to _____ about flying through the clouds when I am asleep in my bed at night.

entries.d: to go down suddenly to the ground
entries.s: Be very careful on the slippery stairs or you will _____ and hurt yourself.

entries.d: to come down from the sky to the ground
entries.s: The aeroplane from London is going to _____ at the airport in ten minutes. Passengers will get off.

entries.d: something you take when you are sick
entries.s: The friendly doctor gave me a bottle of pink _____ to take with a spoon for my bad cold.

entries.d: the blue space above you outside
entries.s: There were no white fluffy clouds in the blue _____ today, just bright sunshine.

entries.d: when there is a lot of wind
entries.s: Hold on tight to your hat! It is very _____ today and the wind is blowing hard.

entries.d: with no light
entries.s: It was very _____ outside because it was midnight and there was no moon in the sky.

entries.d: not old
entries.s: My little baby sister is very _____. She is only three years old and still wears nappies.

entries.d: with nobody else, on your own
entries.s: She was _____ at home all by herself because her parents were out at the cinema.

entries.d: a bag you carry on your back
entries.s: I put my water bottle, my packed lunch, and my homework in my big blue _____ on my back.

entries.d: to take place, to come about
entries.s: What did _____ at school today? Tell me about your day and the test result.

entries.d: to run after someone or something
entries.s: The excited dog started to _____ the scared cat around the garden. The cat ran fast!

entries.d: when the sun is shining
entries.s: It was a warm _____ day with no clouds, so we went to the park for a picnic.

entries.d: a student who speaks for the class
entries.s: We chose Anna as our _____ this school year. She will speak for our class to the headteacher.

entries.d: a system where people can vote
entries.s: In a _____ country like Austria, every adult person can vote in elections for a leader.

entries.d: the leader of a town or city
entries.s: The _____ of our town with a gold chain around his neck opened the new playground on Saturday.

entries.d: about how a country is run
entries.s: We talked about _____ topics like voting and elections and governments in class today.

entries.d: to choose someone in an election
entries.s: The students _____ by raising their hands for a new class speaker every school year.

entries.d: a small thing you press to make something work
entries.s: Press the big red _____ on the front of the machine with your finger to turn it on.

entries.d: a white or grey thing in the sky
entries.s: A big dark grey _____ floated in front of the bright sun and it became dark.

entries.d: the planet we live on
entries.s: People all over _____ — from Africa to Asia to America — want clean drinking water.

entries.d: to stop living
entries.s: The pretty flowers in the pot will _____ and become brown if you don't give them water.

entries.d: a big area with many trees
entries.s: We went for a long quiet walk through the big dark _____ full of tall pine trees and animals.

entries.d: the first part of a text or story
entries.s: Read the short _____ on page 1 before you start chapter 1 of the novel.

entries.d: the area beyond the sky with stars and planets
entries.s: Brave astronauts travel into deep _____ in a big rocket, far from planet Earth.

entries.d: to see something for the first time
entries.s: Did you _____ the beautiful new painting on the wall? It arrived yesterday.

entries.d: to push something with your finger
entries.s: You have to _____ this green button firmly with your finger to start the game on screen.

entries.d: the flat part of a computer or TV you look at
entries.s: The tiny words on the computer _____ were too small to read, so I made them bigger.

entries.d: an exciting thing that happens to you
entries.s: Going into the dark mysterious cave with a torch was a big exciting _____ for me.

entries.d: a person in a story or film
entries.s: My favourite _____ in the book is the brave young girl who saves the village.

entries.d: you say this before telling surprising news
entries.s: _____! I have great news — we are going to the big zoo tomorrow morning!

entries.d: you say this when you want to hear more
entries.s: That sounds very interesting. _____ I want to hear every detail of your story!
